subtitles.original: ちょっと皆さん 各自で回収って形で それだけが申し訳ないんですが
subtitles.english: I'm sorry that you have to go and collect it yourselves,

subtitles.original: お土産ありますので ぜひ気になったらコンビニでプリントしてみてください！
subtitles.english: but it's a souvenir! If you're interested, please try printing it at a convenience store!

subtitles.original: そして コンビニプリント購入できるのは 7月21日17時59分までです！
subtitles.english: Also, the convenience store print will be available for purchase until July 21st at 17:59!

subtitles.original: 概要欄に 写真購入のリンク、多分今マネちゃんがやってくれてると思いますので よろしくお願いします！
subtitles.english: The link to purchase the photo is in the description! My manager is probably putting it up now, so please check it out!

subtitles.original: そしてこの後はですね MVの公開と メンバーシップ限定のアンコールライブありますので
subtitles.english: And after this, we have the MV release and the membership-exclusive encore live...

subtitles.original: そちらも絶対に聴いてくださいね！
subtitles.english: ...so please make sure to tune in to those as well!

subtitles.original: それでは皆さん またお会いしましょう！
subtitles.english: Well then, everyone, see you again!

subtitles.original: 最高の誕生日でした！バイバーイ！
subtitles.english: This was the best birthday ever! Bye-bye!

responses.thoughts:
**Subtitle Generation Process - Kazama Iroha Video Segment**

Okay, here's the breakdown. The task is clear: I need to generate perfectly synchronized subtitles in both Japanese (`og`) and English (`en`) for a specific video segment (00:00-01:16), formatted as a JSON object, and I need to remain SILENT during any pure instrumental music.

First, I need to get the exact timecodes, that's paramount. The video starts with Kazama Iroha talking about convenience store prints. So, I listen to the video very carefully and transcribe the Japanese audio. After that I have to translate each segment in English.

I have to make sure the timecodes are accurate down to the millisecond. I'm noting the beginning and end times for each line, making sure there's a smooth flow. No CC tags or anything clunky, just clean text. Grammar needs to be on point, and the English translation needs to make sense semantically. It needs to read naturally, with perfect semantic continuity.

I then go line by line, aligning the timings, transcribing, and translating. Each segment needs to be perfect:

*   **00:00.000 - 00:04.200**: "ちょっと 皆さん 各自で回収って形で それだけが申し訳ないんですが" ("I'm sorry that you have to go and collect it yourselves,")
*   **00:04.200 - 00:08.500**: "お土産ありますので ぜひ気になったら コンビニでプリントしてみてください！" ("but it's a souvenir! If you're interested, please try printing it at a convenience store!")
*   **00:08.900 - 00:14.400**: "そして コンビニプリント購入できるのは 7月21日17時59分までです！" ("Also, the convenience store print will be available for purchase until July 21st at 17:59!")
*   **00:14.500 - 00:21.800**: "概要欄に 写真購入のリンク、多分今マネちゃんがやってくれてると思いますので よろしくお願いします！" ("The link to purchase the photo is in the description! My manager is probably putting it up now, so please check it out!")
*   **00:22.500 - 00:27.500**: "そしてこの後はですね MVの公開と メンバーシップ限定のアンコールライブありますので" ("And after this, we have the MV release and the membership-exclusive encore live...")
*   **00:27.500 - 00:30.900**: "そちらも絶対に聴いてくださいね！" ("...so please make sure to tune in to those as well!")
*   **00:31.500 - 00:35.300**: "それでは皆さん またお会いしましょう！" ("Well then, everyone, see you again!")
*   **00:35.300 - 00:41.000**: "最高の誕生日でした！バイバーイ！" ("This was the best birthday ever! Bye-bye!")

I double-check everything, listening again, fine-tuning the timings. I make sure the Japanese text is accurate. I check the transition at 00:08.900, at 00:14.500, at 00:22.500, and at 00:27.500. Everything sounds just right. No subtitles after 00:41.000, as instructed.

With timings and translations verified, it's time to build the JSON output. All ready.
